description: Develop with vigorous growth or abundant activity. A rose or forest may 'ruloi'; so may an art or community when activity and production become plentiful. 'welira' (thrive) follows the health of a living subject, while ordinary increase is 'thuroa' (grow).
articulatory_notes: Tap the tongue tip once at the alveolar ridge for 'r' and release into rounded high-back 'u'. Return the tip to the ridge for stressed 'l' and move into rounded middle-height back 'o'. Keep the final high-front 'i' separate rather than blending it with the preceding vowel.
sound_symbolism: A tap and a lateral keep the middle of the word lively, and the separate final 'i' lifts away from the rounded vowel before it. The quick change offers a useful cue for vigorous activity.
usage_notes: Use 'ruloi' intransitively. Abundance belongs to the activity or growth itself; available resources use 'lorua' (abundant), and actual useful yield uses 'poru' (productive).

semantic_domains.activity: a practice or art developing with abundant activity
semantic_domains.community: vigorous growth in a community or language
semantic_domains.nature: abundant growth in plants or a living place
